fundamental_rights: Article 21 — Right to Life and Personal Liberty (includes right to live with dignity)
Article 14 — Right to Equality before Law
Article 15 — Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of sex
Article 39(e) — State shall ensure health and strength of workers and prevent abuse

laws.name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
sections: Section 3 (definition of domestic violence — physical, sexual, verbal, economic)
Section 17 (right to reside in shared household)
laws.summary: Comprehensive law providing protection orders, residence rights, monetary relief, and maintenance to women facing domestic violence.
laws.remedy: File application through Protection Officer, Magistrate Court, or Service Provider NGO

laws.name: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), 2023 / IPC
sections: Section 85/86 BNS (cruelty by husband or relatives — replaces IPC 498A)
laws.summary: Criminal offences for domestic cruelty, dowry harassment, and physical abuse. IPC 498A is a cognisable, non-bailable offence.
laws.remedy: File FIR at police station — police must register, it is a cognisable offence

laws.name: Dowry Prohibition Act, 1961
laws.summary: Prohibits demand or exchange of dowry. Both giving and taking dowry is a criminal offence.
laws.remedy: File complaint with Dowry Prohibition Officer or police

laws.name: Hindu Marriage Act, 1955 / Special Marriage Act, 1954
laws.summary: Provides grounds for divorce and maintenance rights during and after separation.

steps: EMERGENCY: If in immediate danger, call 112 (Emergency) or 1091 (Women's Helpline) immediately
Move to a safe place — stay with family, friends, or a shelter home (Swadhar Greh: 181)
Document all injuries with photographs, medical reports, and hospital records
Save all threatening messages, emails, and voice recordings on a safe device
Contact your nearest Protection Officer (every district has one) for free assistance
File application for Protection Order in Magistrate Court — Protection Officer can help you do this for free
File FIR at the nearest police station under BNS Section 85 (IPC 498A) if cruelty is involved
Apply for maintenance under Section 125 CrPC / BNSS in Family Court
Contact free legal aid: NALSA 15100 or your District Legal Services Authority
